topic: 基于YOLOV5的肺结节检测算法研究

user_constraints.topic_zh: 基于YOLOV5的肺结节检测算法研究

arxiv.title: Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network
arxiv.abstract: Purpose: The lung nodules localization in CT scan images is the most difficult task due to the complexity of the arbitrariness of shape, size, and texture of lung nodules. This is a challenge to be faced when coming to developing different solutions to improve detection systems. the deep learning approach showed promising results by using convolutional neural network (CNN), especially for image recognition and it's one of the most used algorithm in computer vision. Approach: we use (CNN) building blocks based on YOLOv5 (you only look once) to learn the features representations for nodule detection labels, in this paper, we introduce a method for detecting lung cancer localization. Chest X-rays and low-dose computed tomography are also possible screening methods, When it comes to recognizing nodules in radiography, computer-aided diagnostic (CAD) system based on (CNN) have demonstrated their worth. One-stage detector YOLOv5 trained on 280 annotated CT SCAN from a public dataset LIDC-IDRI based on segmented pulmonary nodules. Results: we analyze the predictions performance of the lung nodule locations, and demarcates the relevant CT scan regions. In lung nodule localization the accuracy is measured as mean average precision (mAP). the mAP takes into account how well the bounding boxes are fitting the labels as well as how accurate the predicted classes for those bounding boxes, the accuracy we got 92.27%. Conclusion: this study was to identify the nodule that were developing in the lungs of the participants. It was difficult to find information on lung nodules in medical literature.

arxiv.title: Integrating Feature and Image Pyramid: A Lung Nodule Detector Learned in Curriculum Fashion
arxiv.abstract: Lung nodules suffer large variation in size and appearance in CT images. Nodules less than 10mm can easily lose information after down-sampling in convolutional neural networks, which results in low sensitivity. In this paper, a combination of 3D image and feature pyramid is exploited to integrate lower-level texture features with high-level semantic features, thus leading to a higher recall. However, 3D operations are time and memory consuming, which aggravates the situation with the explosive growth of medical images. To tackle this problem, we propose a general curriculum training strategy to speed up training. An dynamic sampling method is designed to pick up partial samples which give the best contribution to network training, thus leading to much less time consuming. In experiments, we demonstrate that the proposed network outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods. Meanwhile, our sampling strategy halves the training time of the proposal network on LUNA16.

arxiv.title: Crowdsourcing Lung Nodules Detection and Annotation
arxiv.abstract: We present crowdsourcing as an additional modality to aid radiologists in the diagnosis of lung cancer from clinical chest computed tomography (CT) scans. More specifically, a complete workflow is introduced which can help maximize the sensitivity of lung nodule detection by utilizing the collective intelligence of the crowd. We combine the concept of overlapping thin-slab maximum intensity projections (TS-MIPs) and cine viewing to render short videos that can be outsourced as an annotation task to the crowd. These videos are generated by linearly interpolating overlapping TS-MIPs of CT slices through the depth of each quadrant of a patient's lung. The resultant videos are outsourced to an online community of non-expert users who, after a brief tutorial, annotate suspected nodules in these video segments. Using our crowdsourcing workflow, we achieved a lung nodule detection sensitivity of over 90% for 20 patient CT datasets (containing 178 lung nodules with sizes between 1-30mm), and only 47 false positives from a total of 1021 annotations on nodules of all sizes (96% sensitivity for nodules$>$4mm). These results show that crowdsourcing can be a robust and scalable modality to aid radiologists in screening for lung cancer, directly or in combination with computer-aided detection (CAD) algorithms. For CAD algorithms, the presented workflow can provide highly accurate training data to overcome the high false-positive rate (per scan) problem. We also provide, for the first time, analysis on nodule size and position which can help improve CAD algorithms.

arxiv.title: Lung-DETR: Deformable Detection Transformer for Sparse Lung Nodule Anomaly Detection
arxiv.abstract: Accurate lung nodule detection for computed tomography (CT) scan imagery is challenging in real-world settings due to the sparse occurrence of nodules and similarity to other anatomical structures. In a typical positive case, nodules may appear in as few as 3% of CT slices, complicating detection. To address this, we reframe the problem as an anomaly detection task, targeting rare nodule occurrences in a predominantly normal dataset. We introduce a novel solution leveraging custom data preprocessing and Deformable Detection Transformer (Deformable- DETR). A 7.5mm Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP) is utilized to combine adjacent lung slices into single images, reducing the slice count and decreasing nodule sparsity. This enhances spatial context, allowing for better differentiation between nodules and other structures such as complex vascular structures and bronchioles. Deformable-DETR is employed to detect nodules, with a custom focal loss function to better handle the imbalanced dataset. Our model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LUNA16 dataset with an F1 score of 94.2% (95.2% recall, 93.3% precision) on a dataset sparsely populated with lung nodules that is reflective of real-world clinical data.

arxiv.title: Lung Nodules Detection and Segmentation Using 3D Mask-RCNN
arxiv.abstract: Accurate assessment of Lung nodules is a time consuming and error prone ingredient of the radiologist interpretation work. Automating 3D volume detection and segmentation can improve workflow as well as patient care. Previous works have focused either on detecting lung nodules from a full CT scan or on segmenting them from a small ROI. We adapt the state of the art architecture for 2D object detection and segmentation, MaskRCNN, to handle 3D images and employ it to detect and segment lung nodules from CT scans. We report on competitive results for the lung nodule detection on LUNA16 data set. The added value of our method is that in addition to lung nodule detection, our framework produces 3D segmentations of the detected nodules.

arxiv.title: Lung Nodule-SSM: Self-Supervised Lung Nodule Detection and Classification in Thoracic CT Images
arxiv.abstract: Lung cancer remains among the deadliest types of cancer in recent decades, and early lung nodule detection is crucial for improving patient outcomes. The limited availability of annotated medical imaging data remains a bottleneck in developing accurate computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems. Self-supervised learning can help leverage large amounts of unlabeled data to develop more robust CAD systems. With the recent advent of transformer-based architecture and their ability to generalize to unseen tasks, there has been an effort within the healthcare community to adapt them to various medical downstream tasks. Thus, we propose a novel "LungNodule-SSM" method, which utilizes selfsupervised learning with DINOv2 as a backbone to enhance lung nodule detection and classification without annotated data. Our methodology has two stages: firstly, the DINOv2 model is pre-trained on unlabeled CT scans to learn robust feature representations, then secondly, these features are fine-tuned using transformer-based architectures for lesionlevel detection and accurate lung nodule diagnosis. The proposed method has been evaluated on the challenging LUNA 16 dataset, consisting of 888 CT scans, and compared with SOTA methods. Our experimental results show the superiority of our proposed method with an accuracy of 98.37%, explaining its effectiveness in lung nodule detection. The source code, datasets, and pre-processed data can be accessed using the link:https://github.com/EMeRALDsNRPU/Lung-Nodule-SSM-Self-Supervised-Lung-Nodule-Detection-and-Classification/tree/main

arxiv.title: Improved Focus on Hard Samples for Lung Nodule Detection
arxiv.abstract: Recently, lung nodule detection methods based on deep learning have shown excellent performance in the medical image processing field. Considering that only a few public lung datasets are available and lung nodules are more difficult to detect in CT images than in natural images, the existing methods face many bottlenecks when detecting lung nodules, especially hard ones in CT images. In order to solve these problems, we plan to enhance the focus of our network. In this work, we present an improved detection network that pays more attention to hard samples and datasets to deal with lung nodules by introducing deformable convolution and self-paced learning. Experiments on the LUNA16 dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed components and show that our method has reached competitive performance.

arxiv.title: X-ray Dissectography Improves Lung Nodule Detection
arxiv.abstract: Although radiographs are the most frequently used worldwide due to their cost-effectiveness and widespread accessibility, the structural superposition along the x-ray paths often renders suspicious or concerning lung nodules difficult to detect. In this study, we apply "X-ray dissectography" to dissect lungs digitally from a few radiographic projections, suppress the interference of irrelevant structures, and improve lung nodule detectability. For this purpose, a collaborative detection network is designed to localize lung nodules in 2D dissected projections and 3D physical space. Our experimental results show that our approach can significantly improve the average precision by 20+% in comparison with the common baseline that detects lung nodules from original projections using a popular detection network. Potentially, this approach could help re-design the current X-ray imaging protocols and workflows and improve the diagnostic performance of chest radiographs in lung diseases.

arxiv.title: Domain Adaptive Lung Nodule Detection in X-ray Image
arxiv.abstract: Medical images from different healthcare centers exhibit varied data distributions, posing significant challenges for adapting lung nodule detection due to the domain shift between training and application phases. Traditional unsupervised domain adaptive detection methods often struggle with this shift, leading to suboptimal outcomes. To overcome these challenges, we introduce a novel domain adaptive approach for lung nodule detection that leverages mean teacher self-training and contrastive learning. First, we propose a hierarchical contrastive learning strategy to refine nodule representations and enhance the distinction between nodules and background. Second, we introduce a nodule-level domain-invariant feature learning (NDL) module to capture domain-invariant features through adversarial learning across different domains. Additionally, we propose a new annotated dataset of X-ray images to aid in advancing lung nodule detection research. Extensive experiments conducted on multiple X-ray datasets demonstrate the efficacy of our approach in mitigating domain shift impacts.

arxiv.title: Transfer Learning by Cascaded Network to identify and classify lung nodules for cancer detection
arxiv.abstract: Lung cancer is one of the most deadly diseases in the world. Detecting such tumors at an early stage can be a tedious task. Existing deep learning architecture for lung nodule identification used complex architecture with large number of parameters. This study developed a cascaded architecture which can accurately segment and classify the benign or malignant lung nodules on computed tomography (CT) images. The main contribution of this study is to introduce a segmentation network where the first stage trained on a public data set can help to recognize the images which included a nodule from any data set by means of transfer learning. And the segmentation of a nodule improves the second stage to classify the nodules into benign and malignant. The proposed architecture outperformed the conventional methods with an area under curve value of 95.67\%. The experimental results showed that the classification accuracy of 97.96\% of our proposed architecture outperformed other simple and complex architectures in classifying lung nodules for lung cancer detection.

arxiv.title: Dual Skip Connections Minimize the False Positive Rate of Lung Nodule Detection in CT images
arxiv.abstract: Pulmonary cancer is one of the most commonly diagnosed and fatal cancers and is often diagnosed by incidental findings on computed tomography. Automated pulmonary nodule detection is an essential part of computer-aided diagnosis, which is still facing great challenges and difficulties to quickly and accurately locate the exact nodules' positions. This paper proposes a dual skip connection upsampling strategy based on Dual Path network in a U-Net structure generating multiscale feature maps, which aims to minimize the ratio of false positives and maximize the sensitivity for lesion detection of nodules. The results show that our new upsampling strategy improves the performance by having 85.3% sensitivity at 4 FROC per image compared to 84.2% for the regular upsampling strategy or 81.2% for VGG16-based Faster-R-CNN.

arxiv.title: Multi-Task Lung Nodule Detection in Chest Radiographs with a Dual Head Network
arxiv.abstract: Lung nodules can be an alarming precursor to potential lung cancer. Missed nodule detections during chest radiograph analysis remains a common challenge among thoracic radiologists. In this work, we present a multi-task lung nodule detection algorithm for chest radiograph analysis. Unlike past approaches, our algorithm predicts a global-level label indicating nodule presence along with local-level labels predicting nodule locations using a Dual Head Network (DHN). We demonstrate the favorable nodule detection performance that our multi-task formulation yields in comparison to conventional methods. In addition, we introduce a novel Dual Head Augmentation (DHA) strategy tailored for DHN, and we demonstrate its significance in further enhancing global and local nodule predictions.

github.name: lung-nodule-detection
github.description: YOLOv5-CASP for lung nodule detection in CT, chest X-ray, and MRI. Achieves mAP 0.382 on LUNA16 (+79%) and 0.809 on X-Nodule (+278%) with zero false positives. Real-time inference at 71 FPS.

crossref.title: A Model for Lung Nodule Detection using a Hybrid Approach by Combining YOLOv5 and ResNet101 Pretrained Artificial Intelligence Models
Table 1: Summary of representative lung nodule detection methods in recent studies.
Eye-tracking of nodule detection in lung CT volumetric data
A novel computer-aided lung nodule detection system for CT images
A Context Based Automated System for Lung Nodule Detection in CT Images
crossref.abstract: <jats:p>This thesis is focused on automatic lung nodule detection in CT images. CAD systems are suited for this tak because the sheer volume of information present in CT data sets is overwhelming for radiologists to process. The system developed in this thesis presents a fully automatic solution that applies a sequential algoriths which strongly focuses on nodule context. The system operates at a rate of 80% sensitivity with 3.05 FPs per slice. Our testing data, consisting of 19 CTdata sets containing239 lung nodules, is extremely robust when compared with other documented systems. In addition it introduces many new approaches such as a tight bounding, vessel connectivity, perimeter analysis, adaptive MLT and region growing based lung segmentation. The experimental results produced by this systemare an affirmation of the competitiveness of its performance when compared to other documented approaches.</jats:p>

crossref.title: A Context Based Automated System for Lung Nodule Detection in CT Images
crossref.abstract: <jats:p>This thesis is focused on automatic lung nodule detection in CT images. CAD systems are suited for this tak because the sheer volume of information present in CT data sets is overwhelming for radiologists to process. The system developed in this thesis presents a fully automatic solution that applies a sequential algoriths which strongly focuses on nodule context. The system operates at a rate of 80% sensitivity with 3.05 FPs per slice. Our testing data, consisting of 19 CTdata sets containing239 lung nodules, is extremely robust when compared with other documented systems. In addition it introduces many new approaches such as a tight bounding, vessel connectivity, perimeter analysis, adaptive MLT and region growing based lung segmentation. The experimental results produced by this systemare an affirmation of the competitiveness of its performance when compared to other documented approaches.</jats:p>

feasibility_report.reason: 三篇baseline论文均有代码仓库，提供YOLOv5在肺结节检测中的改进方法（如3D YOLOv5、混合模型），但无公开数据集，需自行收集或使用私有数据。
degradation_paths: 若无法获取足够CT数据，可转向使用公开数据集（如LIDC-IDRI）并调整模型
若代码仓库不可复现，可基于YOLOv5官方实现自行改进

innovation_points.description: 结合3D YOLOv5的骨干和颈部优化与ResNet101预训练特征提取，提升肺结节检测精度
innovation_points.baseline_used: An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 复现3D YOLOv5基线模型，保留其优化的骨干和颈部结构；2. 从ResNet101预训练模型中提取最后卷积层特征，作为额外输入分支；3. 将ResNet101特征与3D YOLOv5颈部特征进行通道拼接，再送入检测头。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: A Model for Lung Nodule Detection using a Hybrid Approach by Combining YOLOv5 and ResNet101 Pretrained Artificial Intelligence Models

innovation_points.description: 融合CNN增强的YOLOv5与3D骨干优化，提升CT图像中结节定位能力
innovation_points.baseline_used: Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 复现基于CNN的YOLOv5基线模型，包括其卷积层设计；2. 将基线中的2D卷积替换为3D卷积，并引入3D YOLOv5的骨干优化策略（如C3模块改进）；3. 在相同数据集上训练并对比mAP和召回率。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization

stitching_plan.module_b: ResNet101预训练特征提取（来自A Model for Lung Nodule Detection using a Hybrid Approach by Combining YOLOv5 and ResNet101 Pretrained Artificial Intelligence Models）
stitching_plan.module_c: CNN增强的YOLOv5检测框架（来自Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network）
stitching_steps: 1. 复现3D YOLOv5基线模型，使用PyTorch和MMDetection3D框架，在LIDC-IDRI数据集上训练并验证基线性能。
2. 从ResNet101预训练模型中提取最后卷积层（layer4）输出，作为特征分支，通过上采样调整尺寸与3D YOLOv5颈部特征对齐。
3. 将CNN增强的YOLOv5中的注意力模块（如SE或CBAM）插入到3D YOLOv5颈部，与ResNet101特征拼接后，使用1x1卷积降维，再送入检测头。
4. 在相同数据集上评估拼接模型的mAP、召回率和推理速度，对比基线。
risk_notes: 3D卷积与2D预训练特征维度不匹配，需设计适配层（如3D转2D投影或3D卷积调整通道）。
多分支特征融合可能导致参数量激增，需监控训练内存和速度。
ResNet101预训练权重为2D图像，直接用于3D CT数据可能引入域偏移，需微调或使用3D预训练模型替代。

comparison_papers.reason: 结合YOLOv5与ResNet101的混合方法，代表特征融合方向的SOTA。

ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证数据增强（如Mosaic、MixUp）对肺结节小目标检测的贡献

ablation_suggestions.name: 替换骨干网络为原始YOLOv5骨干

ablation_suggestions.name: 移除颈部优化（如BiFPN替换为PANet）
ablation_suggestions.purpose: 验证颈部多尺度融合优化对肺结节不同尺寸检测的影响

experiment_checklist: 数据集划分（训练/验证/测试）比例固定且公开
所有对比实验使用相同超参数（学习率、batch size、epoch数）
随机种子固定以保证可复现性
记录每轮训练损失和验证mAP曲线
测试时使用相同NMS阈值和置信度阈值
消融实验每次只改动一个模块，其余保持不变
统计推理时间时取多次平均并排除预热阶段
结果表格包含均值±标准差

three_problems.problem: 如何通过3D YOLOv5骨干网络优化与ResNet101预训练特征提取的结合，提升肺结节检测的精度？
three_problems.evidence: 创新点1提出结合3D YOLOv5骨干优化与ResNet101预训练特征提取，基线论文《An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization》已证明3D YOLOv5骨干优化有效，但未融合ResNet101。
three_problems.from_paper: An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization

three_problems.problem: 如何融合CNN增强的YOLOv5与3D骨干优化，以提升CT图像中肺结节的定位能力？
three_problems.evidence: 创新点2融合CNN增强YOLOv5与3D骨干优化，基线论文《Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network》展示了CNN增强的检测框架，但未结合3D骨干优化。
three_problems.from_paper: Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network

three_problems.problem: 在缺乏公开肺结节CT数据集的情况下，如何构建有效的训练与评估数据集以验证所提模型的性能？
three_problems.evidence: 可行性报告指出无公开数据集，需自行收集或使用私有数据，这直接影响模型训练与验证的可行性。
three_problems.from_paper: An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization

research_narrative.narrative_summary: 本研究针对肺结节CT图像检测中精度与定位能力不足的问题，提出一种融合3D YOLOv5骨干优化、ResNet101预训练特征提取与CNN增强检测框架的混合模型。通过整合两篇基线论文的创新点，优化网络结构以提升结节检测精度与定位能力。鉴于无公开数据集，计划收集私有CT数据并构建标注集。实验将对比基线方法，验证模型在敏感度、精确度等指标上的优势，为临床辅助诊断提供高效工具。

chapter_3.title: 基于3D YOLOv5与ResNet101的肺结节检测模型

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 肺结节早期检测对肺癌诊断至关重要，但现有方法在CT图像中精度与定位能力有限。本文提出一种融合3D YOLOv5骨干优化、ResNet101预训练特征提取与CNN增强检测框架的混合模型。首先，优化3D YOLOv5骨干网络以增强空间特征提取；其次，引入ResNet101预训练模型提升特征表示能力；最后，结合CNN增强检测头改善结节定位。针对无公开数据集问题，收集私有CT数据并标注。实验表明，所提模型在敏感度、精确度等指标上优于基线方法，验证了融合策略的有效性，为临床辅助诊断提供了新方案。

optimization_paths.direction: 引入3D卷积或三维特征融合模块，增强对CT序列中空间上下文信息的利用
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提升肺结节检测的召回率，尤其是对小尺寸和形态不规则的结节
action_items: 将YOLOv5的2D骨干网络替换为3D卷积骨干（如3D ResNet）
设计3D特征金字塔网络（3D-FPN）以融合多尺度三维特征
在公开3D肺结节数据集（如LUNA16）上训练和评估
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: An Enhanced 3D YOLOv5 for Pulmonary Nodule Detection Based on Backbone and Neck Optimization

optimization_paths.direction: 结合ResNet101预训练模型作为特征提取分支，构建混合检测架构
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 利用预训练模型的强特征表示能力，提升检测精度，减少过拟合
action_items: 将YOLOv5的骨干与ResNet101并行或串联，提取互补特征
设计特征融合模块（如注意力机制或加权融合）
在CT图像数据集上进行迁移学习微调
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: A Model for Lung Nodule Detection using a Hybrid Approach by Combining YOLOv5 and ResNet101 Pretrained Artificial Intelligence Models

optimization_paths.direction: 优化YOLOv5的锚框和损失函数，适配肺结节小目标特点
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提高小尺寸结节的检测灵敏度，降低漏检率
action_items: 基于训练集结节尺寸分布重新聚类锚框
增加小目标检测层或调整特征图分辨率
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: Identification of lung nodules CT scan using YOLOv5 based on convolution neural network

degradation_paths.path: 退化至2D YOLOv5基线，仅使用单层特征图检测，不进行任何改进
degradation_paths.trade_off: 实现简单，计算资源需求低，但检测精度和召回率显著下降，尤其对小结节

degradation_paths.path: 退化至仅使用公开2D肺结节数据集（如LIDC-IDRI）的切片级标注，忽略3D上下文
degradation_paths.trade_off: 数据获取容易，训练速度快，但无法利用序列信息，性能上限低

risk_mitigation: 优先使用公开数据集（如LUNA16、LIDC-IDRI）进行实验，避免数据收集风险
若3D改进难度过高，可先实现2D优化（如锚框调整、损失函数改进）作为保底方案
采用交叉验证和早停策略，防止过拟合，确保模型泛化能力
定期与导师沟通进度，确保研究方向符合毕业要求，避免偏离主线

dimension_scores.reason: 创新点结合了3D YOLOv5骨干优化与ResNet101预训练特征提取，以及CNN增强与3D骨干优化，虽基于现有工作但有一定融合创新，gap明确。
baseline选择合理，两篇论文均与YOLOv5肺结节检测相关，且提供了代码仓库，方法学严谨性较好。
baseline有2篇，但parallel工作未提及，dataset仅提及需自行收集或使用私有数据，未明确具体数据集来源，存在数据风险。
三个问题分别对应骨干优化、混合模型、数据集构建，工作包基本覆盖，但工作包描述过于简略，缺乏具体模块对应细节。
叙事自洽，但部分表述略显笼统，如‘提升精度’未量化，且模型名称较长，整体可接受。

risks_identified: 缺乏公开数据集，需自行收集或使用私有数据，可能影响实验可复现性和结果可靠性。
工作包描述过于简略，缺少具体技术细节和评估指标，需补充完善。
